name: IDF 36th Division (Ga'ash)
detail: One of the primary armored divisions deployed in the Gaza ground operation. Participated in the northern Gaza campaign and later the Khan Younis offensive. Commanded multiple brigade combat teams operating in urban terrain.

name: IDF 98th Division (Fire Formation — Paratroops)
detail: Elite paratroop division that played a central role in the Khan Younis ground offensive in January–April 2024 and subsequent Rafah operations. Led several high-profile operations including attempts to locate Sinwar and senior Hamas leaders.

name: IDF 162nd Division (Steel Formation — Armored)
detail: Armored division deployed in the northern Gaza campaign. Led the advance into Shejaiya and eastern Gaza City neighborhoods. Suffered significant casualties during urban combat including from Hamas anti-tank missiles.

name: Givati Brigade (Infantry)
detail: Elite infantry brigade that participated in the initial ground offensive in northern Gaza. Fought in the Shejaiya neighborhood of Gaza City and later in southern Gaza operations. Sustained significant casualties.

name: Golani Brigade (Infantry)
detail: Israel's most storied infantry brigade, deployed extensively in the Gaza ground operation. Suffered significant losses including multiple company commanders killed. Participated in operations across northern, central, and southern Gaza.

name: F-35I Adir (IAF)
detail: Israel Air Force's most advanced strike aircraft; flew thousands of combat sorties over Gaza. Used for precision strikes on Hamas high-value targets including senior commanders. Demonstrated combat capabilities against an actual adversary for the first time.

name: F-16C/D Barak / Sufa Fleet (IAF)
detail: Workhorse of the Israeli Air Force strike campaign. F-16 jets delivered the bulk of precision munitions in the Gaza campaign. Used for both strategic strikes and close air support to ground forces.

name: Merkava Mk 4 Main Battle Tank
detail: Israel's most advanced main battle tank, deployed extensively in the Gaza ground operation. Equipped with Trophy active protection system (APS) which intercepted many Hamas anti-tank missiles. Some tanks still destroyed by Kornet and Tandem RPG attacks in dense urban terrain.

name: Caterpillar D9 Military Bulldozer
detail: Armored engineering bulldozer used extensively to clear roads of IEDs, demolish buildings concealing Hamas fighters, and excavate tunnel entrances. Dozens deployed in Gaza. Hamas targeted them with anti-tank missiles; crew compartment provides significant protection.

name: Hermes 900 (Kochav) MALE UAV
detail: Medium-altitude long-endurance UAV providing persistent surveillance and strike capability over Gaza. Equipped with Spike missiles for precision strikes on Hamas targets. Used for both ISR and direct action missions throughout the campaign.

name: Heron TP (Eitan) Strategic UAV
detail: Israel's largest and most capable UAV, deployed for strategic reconnaissance over Gaza and the broader region. Capable of long-duration missions monitoring Hamas command networks. Used for targeting intelligence and battle damage assessment.

name: Iron Dome (Kippat Barzel)
detail: Israel's short-range rocket and artillery intercept system. During the Gaza war, multiple Iron Dome batteries deployed around southern Israeli communities and the Tel Aviv area. Intercepted approximately 90% of rockets calculated to hit populated areas. Complemented by David's Sling and Arrow 3 for longer-range threats.

name: Arrow 3 (Chetz 3) Ballistic Missile Defense
detail: Israel's exo-atmospheric missile defense system designed to intercept ballistic missiles above the atmosphere. Used to intercept Iranian ballistic missiles during the April 13–14, 2024 direct Iranian attack. First combat use of the Arrow 3 system.

name: David's Sling (Magic Wand)
detail: Medium-to-long range air defense system designed to intercept cruise missiles and medium-range rockets. Used during both Hezbollah and Iran attacks. Integrates with the multi-layer Israeli air defense network.

name: Sa'ar 5-Class Corvettes (IDF Navy)
detail: Israel's principal surface combatants, deployed offshore Gaza to enforce the naval blockade. Armed with Harpoon anti-ship missiles, Barak air defense, and 76mm guns. Used for naval gunfire support and to prevent Hamas maritime infiltration.

name: Shaldag Unit (IAF SF / Unit 5101)
detail: Israeli Air Force special forces unit specializing in target designation, deep reconnaissance, and direct action. Operated inside Gaza to designate targets for air strikes. Participated in hostage rescue planning and operations.

name: Sayeret Matkal (General Staff Reconnaissance Unit)
detail: Israel's most elite commando unit, equivalent to Delta Force or SAS. Participated in hostage rescue operations and intelligence-gathering missions inside Gaza. Unit is the IDF's primary direct-action counter-terrorism force.

name: Nahal Brigade (Infantry)
detail: Light infantry brigade deployed in the Gaza ground operation. Participated in operations in both northern and central Gaza. The brigade includes infantry and reconnaissance battalions that served extensively in the urban combat environment.

name: Unit 8200 (SIGINT / Cyber)
detail: Israel's SIGINT and cyber intelligence unit, equivalent to the NSA. Provided critical intelligence for targeting throughout the Gaza campaign. Also investigated for use of AI-assisted targeting systems (Lavender, Gospel, Where's Daddy?) that raised ethical questions about automated kill decisions.

name: Spike Anti-Tank / Multi-Purpose Missile System
detail: Family of Israeli precision-guided missiles used extensively in Gaza, both by ground forces against fortified positions and tunnels, and by aircraft/UAVs for targeted strikes on Hamas leaders and infrastructure. Spike NLOS (non-line-of-sight) used for longer-range precision fire.
